headline: Regulatory action against frontier models moved to the center of the quarter as GPT-5.6 and Anthropic's Fable faced bans and a federal recall.
body: The window's sharpest shift is regulatory: GPT-5.6 and Fable were banned, and a federal recall of Anthropic's Fable and Mythos models surfaced at the Big Technology AI Summit, framing model regulation against ongoing AI infrastructure and compute buildout [2][7]. Reviews of the GPT-5.6 Sol, Terra, and Luna lineup found competent but unremarkable gains alongside concerning cyber capabilities measured against the preparedness framework and cyber critical threshold [0]. Tooling momentum continued in agent harnesses, Claude Code plugins and skills, and workflow automation, with Google Antigravity and Gemini Canvas extending file handling and code generation [1][3][10][11]. On the research side, Dwarkesh Patel probed the next training paradigm beyond scaling, covering reinforcement learning and test-time compute, while new technique entrants such as posterior collapse, parameterization, and model cascading broke in [8].
